# Stream consumer

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/stream_consumer_story_test.exs`](../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/stream_consumer_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**3 scenarios.**

## Scenario: a consumer reads an entry and crashes before ack; the SAME branded id is re-delivered

- **Given** a stream with two EVT records and a consumer group reading from the head
- **When** a consumer reads both but the handler crashes-before-ack on one (an {:error, _} verdict leaves it un-acked)
- **Then** the un-acked entry is RE-DELIVERED -- the SAME branded id returns (never an ack-everything no-op)
- **And** the acked entry is NOT re-delivered (only the un-acked one survives in the PEL)

## Scenario: a restarted consumer drains its OWN un-acked backlog first, then reads new (drain-PEL-first)

- **Given** a consumer that read its entries, left them un-acked, then was KILLED (they sit in its PEL)
- **When** a NEW tail entry is appended AFTER the crash and the consumer restarts with the same name
- **Then** the restarted consumer handles its OWN prior un-acked backlog FIRST (by branded id), before the new tail

## Scenario: a polyglot reader sees the same group state; the stored id field is the canonical receipt

- **Given** a stream with one EVT record appended via the BEAM writer, returning the branded receipt
- **When** the entry is read with RAW XREADGROUP through the bare Connector (no Stream/StreamConsumer helpers)
- **Then** the entry's stored id field equals the branded receipt -- a non-BEAM client recovers the canonical id
- **And** a raw XACK through the bare Connector settles the entry against the SAME group state (one group, not two)
